accurate.why: All claims match the bill text or accurate context (DOE as administering agency, IIJA origin) verified via search.

no_hallucinations.why: Naming the Secretary of Energy/DOE is accurate context, not fabrication; no false claims present.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose to reauthorize and revise the RMUC cybersecurity program.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes the bill amends Section 40124 of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, replacing existing program language.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures eligibility limits (e.g., investor-owned under 4M MWh, 6-entity partnership) and priority criteria.

effective_dates.why: Notes the FY2026-2030 appropriations period, the only timing provision specified.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers establishment, eligible entities, awards, objectives, priorities, FOIA exemption, and appropriations.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive tone throughout with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent across logically grouped paragraphs.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition while covering the operative content.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the IIJA, the RMUC Program, and DOE as administering agency.

correct_figures.why: The $250,000,000, FY2026-2030, 4,000,000 MWh, and 6-entity figures all match the bill text.
